leagueBriefing: The Miami Open main draw kicked off with a mix of veteran stability and notable upsets, as French youngster Elsa Jacquemot earned a career-defining win while heavy hitters like Beatriz Haddad Maia suffered early exits.

games.headline: Jacquemot outlasts Vidmanova in three-hour Miami marathon
games.brief: In a grueling physical battle that spanned over three hours, French youngster Elsa Jacquemot showed incredible grit to overturn a first-set deficit against Darja Vidmanova. The win marks a significant milestone for the OL-affiliated player as she navigated a tense final set to secure her first-ever WTA 1000 victory.
bullets: Elsa Jacquemot rallied from a set down to win 6-7(5), 7-5, 7-5.
The match lasted 3 hours and 4 minutes.
Jacquemot secured the decisive break at 5-5 in both the second and third sets.
This victory represents Jacquemot's first main draw win at the WTA 1000 level.
games.context: A breakthrough win for the rising French star, signaling her potential on the game's biggest stages.

games.headline: Tomljanovic clinical in straight-sets victory over Waltert
games.brief: Ajla Tomljanovic looked sharp in her Miami opener, using her powerful baseline game to keep Simona Waltert on the defensive. The Australian was particularly effective on serve, facing minimal pressure throughout the match to advance comfortably to the second round.
bullets: Tomljanovic won 78 out of 143 total points (54.5%).
Simona Waltert struggled with her second serve, winning only 31% of those points.
The match was completed in 1 hour and 51 minutes.
Tomljanovic converted 3 of 11 break point opportunities while saving 5 of 6.
games.context: Tomljanovic builds momentum as she looks to return to the top tier of the rankings after recent injury struggles.

games.headline: Linette storms back with bagel set to dismiss Gracheva
games.brief: After a slow start that saw her drop the first set, Magda Linette completely took over her match against Varvara Gracheva. The Pole found another gear in the final two sets, punctuating her victory with a dominant third-set bagel to set up a blockbuster second-round clash with Iga Swiatek.
bullets: Magda Linette won 12 of the final 14 games in the 2-6, 6-2, 6-0 victory.
Varvara Gracheva was held to zero games in the deciding set.
Linette won 75% of her first-serve points in the final two sets.
The win sets up an all-Polish match against world No. 1 Iga Swiatek.
games.context: The victory sets up a highly anticipated 'Polish Derby' in the next round.

games.headline: Kessler battles past Frech to continue American surge
games.brief: McCartney Kessler continues to make waves on the WTA tour, coming from behind to defeat the experienced Magdalena Frech. Kessler’s aggressive shot-making wore down Frech in the humid Miami conditions, earning the American a hard-fought spot in the next round.
bullets: McCartney Kessler rallied for a 2-6, 6-4, 6-2 victory.
Kessler won 58% of the total points in the final set.
Magdalena Frech was broken four times during the match.
Kessler won 82% of her first-serve points during the second set comeback.
games.context: Kessler continues her breakout year with another victory at the 1000 level.

games.headline: Veteran Siegemund handles teen challenger Marcinko
games.brief: Laura Siegemund’s variety and experience proved too much for the talented teenager Petra Marcinko. In a battle of generations, the German veteran used her slice and net play to disrupt Marcinko's rhythm, securing a straight-sets win in her Miami opener.
bullets: Laura Siegemund won in straight sets, 6-4, 6-4.
Siegemund saved all three break points she faced in the first set.
Petra Marcinko, a former junior world No. 1, committed 28 unforced errors.
The match lasted 1 hour and 42 minutes.
games.context: Siegemund’s tactical prowess continues to make her a dangerous floater in the draw.

games.headline: Boulter survives tiebreak scare to advance in Miami
games.brief: British No. 1 Katie Boulter passed a stern test against Spain's Jessica Bouzas Maneiro. After a marathon opening set tiebreak that saw multiple set points for both sides, Boulter’s experience shone through in the second set to close out the victory.
bullets: Katie Boulter won 7-6(9), 6-4.
The first set tiebreak featured 20 total points.
Boulter fired 6 aces throughout the match.
Jessica Bouzas Maneiro converted only 2 of her 7 break point chances.
games.context: Boulter looks to build on her career-best form after her recent title in San Diego.

games.headline: Cirstea dominant in straight-sets victory over Zhang
games.brief: Sorana Cirstea continued her love affair with the Miami courts, delivering a disciplined performance to defeat Shuai Zhang. Cirstea’s flat hitting proved effective on the hard courts, as she broke Zhang's serve early in both sets to control the tempo of the match.
bullets: Sorana Cirstea won 6-3, 6-4.
Cirstea won 80% of her first-serve points.
Shuai Zhang was unable to capitalize on any of her three break point opportunities.
The match was completed in a brisk 1 hour and 15 minutes.
games.context: Cirstea looks to repeat her deep run in Miami from previous seasons.

games.headline: Sonmez stuns Haddad Maia in massive Miami upset
games.brief: In the shock of the opening round, Turkish qualifier Zeynep Sonmez played the match of her life to eliminate the high-seeded Beatriz Haddad Maia. Sonmez showed no nerves, attacking the Brazilian's second serve and dominating the baseline exchanges to secure the biggest win of her career.
bullets: Zeynep Sonmez won in straight sets, 6-3, 6-2.
Haddad Maia committed 32 unforced errors against only 12 winners.
Sonmez broke the Brazilian's serve five times during the match.
This was Sonmez's first victory over a top-20 opponent.
games.context: A major blow to the seeded players as Haddad Maia exits much earlier than expected.

games.headline: Blinkova rallies to deepen Kenin's early-season woes
games.brief: Former Miami champion Sofia Kenin's struggles continued as she was unable to hold a set lead against Anna Blinkova. After Kenin took a tight opening set, Blinkova raised her aggression and accuracy, while Kenin's game unraveled with a string of unforced errors in the final two sets.
bullets: Anna Blinkova won 3-6, 6-1, 6-3.
Blinkova fired 9 aces, a high mark for her so far this season.
Kenin was broken six times after the first set.
The match turned in the second set where Blinkova won 26 of 37 points played.
games.context: Kenin continues to search for the form that once saw her win a Grand Slam title.

games.headline: Osorio breezes past Siniakova in straight sets
games.brief: Colombia's Camila Osorio put on a masterclass of counter-punching to defeat the experienced Katerina Siniakova. Osorio's court coverage and ability to redirect pace left Siniakova frustrated, as the Czech player struggled to find a way through Osorio's defense in a one-sided affair.
bullets: Camila Osorio won 6-1, 6-4 in just 1 hour and 19 minutes.
Osorio won 68.8% of the total points in the opening set.
Siniakova committed 5 double faults and won only 42.9% of her service points.
Osorio saved 5 of the 6 break points she faced during the match.
games.context: Osorio’s dominant start makes her a player to watch in the early rounds in Miami.
